title: TasteKit Constitution v1
description: The locked artifact contract for a single user's taste profile. Read by all TasteKit runtime adapters (Claude Code, Hermes, future Codex). Versioning: schema_version is the wire-incompatibility breakpoint; generator_version is semver of the producing TasteKit. Forward-compat additions go in the open `extensions` slot. Readers MUST ignore unknown keys inside `extensions` without failing.

schema_version.description: Wire format version. Bump to constitution.v2 only on incompatible change (renamed/removed required field, type change, broken enum).

generated_at.description: UTC ISO 8601 with millisecond precision (3 digits): YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.sssZ. Matches Date.prototype.toISOString() output exactly. Calendar validity (e.g. rejecting 2026-02-30) cannot be expressed in portable JSON Schema and is enforced only by Zod's runtime refinement layer.

generator_version.description: Semver 2.0.0 of the TasteKit version that produced this artifact. Leading zeros forbidden. Supports prerelease (-alpha.1) and build metadata (+sha.deadbeef). Bumping is internal to the producer; does not change wire format (that's schema_version).

user_scope.description: v1 supports single_user only. Multi-user comes in v2.

principles.description: Ordered principles (priority 1 = highest). At least one required; cap at 20 to keep the artifact useful for prompting.

trace_map.description: Provenance: maps principle_id and section keys to source dimensions / interview turns. Optional but strongly recommended.

extensions.description: Forward-compat slot for Phase 2 features (memory hints, drift thresholds, perf budgets). Readers MUST tolerate unknown keys here. Reserved keys (informational): `memory`, `drift`, `perf`, `x-<runtime>-*`.

id.description: Stable principle identifier. Slug-form, derived from statement (e.g., "clarity_over_completeness"). Numeric-only IDs (`principle_0`) are not allowed by the producer; readers should still accept any non-empty string for forward compatibility with hand-edited artifacts.

priority.description: Priority (1 = highest). Within a constitution, priorities should be unique and form a 1..N sequence.

statement.description: The principle statement. One sentence imperative.

rationale.description: Why this principle matters. Each principle's rationale must be distinct from the others (validator-enforced at extraction time).

applies_to.description: Tags: skill_ids, domains, or contexts. Use ["*"] for global.

examples_good.description: Positive examples specific to this principle. Must not duplicate examples_good of another principle.

examples_bad.description: Negative examples specific to this principle.

formatting_rules.description: Formatting preferences (markdown style, code-block usage, bullet vs prose).

never_do.description: Absolute prohibitions. Agent must never take these actions under any circumstances.

must_escalate.description: Actions requiring human approval before proceeding. Distinct from never_do; the agent pauses and asks rather than refusing outright.
